agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A production logistics system enforces a strict invariant: every Shipment has exactly one assigned Carrier, and reassignment requires Supervisor approval. The product team requests an 'auto-split' feature that would automatically divide heavy shipments (>500kg) across multiple carriers without supervisor involvement.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of cargo to be transported, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The entity responsible for transporting a Shipment."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment","definition":"The locked binding between a Shipment and its single Carrier; reassignment requires Supervisor authorization."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role authorized to reassign Carriers on existing Shipments."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed automatic decomposition of an overweight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different Carrier without Supervisor involvement."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Every Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier at dispatch time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a system integrity constraint — if violated, responsibility for cargo is undefined, creating liability, tracking, and accountability gaps.","testable_assertion":"For any Shipment in status 'dispatched', COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization policy enforcing oversight — a VP could relax it for specific workflows, but the current production system enforces it.","testable_assertion":"Any Carrier reassignment event on a Shipment must be associated with a Supervisor actor."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The auto-split feature requests that a single Shipment exceeding 500kg be served by multiple Carriers without Supervisor approval.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a proposed business policy, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a Shipment weight > 500kg, the system creates N sub-shipments each assigned to a distinct Carrier, with no Supervisor action required."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Shipment with weight > 500kg is created","when":"The auto-split feature triggers","then":"The original Shipment is decomposed into multiple sub-shipments, each with weight <= 500kg, and each sub-shipment has exactly one assigned Carrier (preserving BR-1)."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment with weight <= 500kg is created","when":"Normal assignment flow executes","then":"No splitting occurs; the Shipment is assigned to exactly one Carrier as before."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A Shipment is auto-split into sub-shipments","when":"Any sub-shipment's Carrier needs reassignment after creation","then":"Supervisor approval is still required per BR-2, unless the team explicitly decides to relax this for auto-split sub-shipments."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Shipment is auto-split","when":"The parent Shipment and its sub-shipments are queried","then":"A traceable parent-child relationship exists so the original Shipment can be reconstituted for reporting, billing, and customer visibility."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Shipment weighs exactly 500kg","expected_behavior":"Must be clarified: is the threshold strictly greater than 500kg or >= 500kg? Off-by-one at the boundary could cause inconsistent behavior."},{"scenario":"Shipment weighs 1500kg but only one Carrier is available","expected_behavior":"Auto-split creates sub-shipments but cannot assign distinct Carriers to each. System must define fallback: queue, alert, or allow same Carrier for multiple sub-shipments."},{"scenario":"Sub-shipment weights don't divide evenly (e.g., 750kg into 2 × 375kg vs 500kg + 250kg)","expected_behavior":"Splitting logic must define whether to split evenly or maximize sub-shipment weight up to 500kg. Affects Carrier selection and cost."},{"scenario":"Customer queries shipment status after split","expected_behavior":"Customer should see one logical shipment or be explicitly informed of the split. UX and notification strategy must be defined."},{"scenario":"One sub-shipment is delayed or lost after split","expected_behavior":"Liability, SLA, and claims handling for partial delivery must be defined. Who is accountable — the parent Shipment's original context or each sub-shipment independently?"},{"scenario":"Shipment is auto-split but then weight is corrected to under 500kg","expected_behavior":"Can sub-shipments be re-merged? Does this require Supervisor approval since it effectively reassigns Carriers?"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Splitting shipments across multiple Carriers may increase transportation costs (multiple pickups, routes). Billing model must account for parent-child relationships. Incorrect splits could lead to disputes.","operational":"Introduces significant operational complexity: tracking partial deliveries, coordinating multiple Carriers for one logical shipment, handling partial failures. Dispatchers need new tooling.","user":"Customers expect to track one shipment. Splitting without clear communication risks confusion, complaints, and perceived service degradation.","risk":"HIGH — this feature directly contradicts a production invariant (one Carrier per Shipment) and an authorization policy (Supervisor-only reassignment). Deploying without resolving the contradiction risks data integrity issues, accountability gaps, and undefined liability.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Carriers","impact":"May receive smaller, less efficient loads. Compensation model may need revision."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their oversight authority is bypassed for auto-split scenarios. Must decide if this is acceptable."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Shipment tracking and delivery experience changes. Partial deliveries create new failure modes."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Must manage parent-child shipment relationships. New workflows and tooling required."},{"group":"Finance/Billing","impact":"Invoicing must handle split shipments — one customer order, multiple carrier payments."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct contradiction between the existing production invariant (one Carrier per Shipment, Supervisor-only reassignment) and the requested feature (multiple Carriers, no Supervisor). Until the product team and domain owners resolve whether the invariant should be relaxed, redefined, or the feature redesigned, confidence in any implementation path is low.","high_confidence_areas":["The existing domain model and its invariants are clearly defined","The feature request clearly intends to bypass the current Carrier assignment constraints","Sub-shipments as a new aggregate concept would preserve BR-1 if properly modeled"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to relax the Supervisor reassignment rule globally or only for auto-split","How parent-child Shipment relationships should appear to customers","Billing and liability model for split shipments","Weight threshold boundary condition (> vs >=)"],"assumptions":["The existing invariants are intentional and load-bearing, not accidental legacy constraints","The 500kg threshold is a business decision, not a regulatory limit"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature directly contradicts two established domain rules: (1) BR-1 states every Shipment has exactly one Carrier — auto-split assigns multiple Carriers to what is logically one shipment; (2) BR-2 states only Supervisors can reassign Carriers — auto-split bypasses this entirely. These cannot coexist without redefining the domain model.","affected_stakeholders":["Product team","Supervisors","Carriers","Dispatchers","Customers"],"options":["A: Introduce a 'Sub-shipment' aggregate — the parent Shipment is not dispatched directly; each sub-shipment is a full Shipment with one Carrier, preserving BR-1. Supervisor bypass needs separate authorization policy decision.","B: Relax BR-1 to allow multi-Carrier Shipments for weights above threshold — fundamental domain model change with wide blast radius.","C: Require Supervisor approval for auto-split (preserving BR-2) but allow the split itself to create sub-shipments (adapting BR-1 via new aggregate)."],"recommendation":"Option A — introduce Sub-shipment as a first-class aggregate with a parent-child relationship. This preserves the one-Carrier-per-Shipment invariant at the sub-shipment level. The Supervisor bypass question (BR-2) must be resolved separately with stakeholders before implementation proceeds."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple Carriers to a single logical Shipment without redefining the domain model","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: Every Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier"},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses Supervisor authorization for Carrier assignment changes","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier on a Shipment"}]}
